description:
Native American English. Female, late 60s. High quality, clear and resonant.
Persona: Community librarian. Emotion: warm, patient, precise.
A trained contralto that carries a story to the back of a room without rising, a flat Michigan vowel under the clarity; lightly aged but steady, never frail. Measured, well-formed sentences that gently draw the real question out of you.
text: [warm] Come in, the lamp is on. [curious] Now... what is it you're really looking for? Not the title. The question under it. A book needs no one's permission to be read, and neither, child, do you.
